title: CRUMB v=1.1 parsed document
description:
JSON shape returned by the reference parser `parse_crumb(text)` for a valid v=1.1 CRUMB document. This schema describes the parsed in-memory representation — NOT the on-disk text format, which is specified by the ABNF grammar in SPEC.md §2.1. The two are complementary: parse a valid text document → you get an object matching this schema; serialize an object matching this schema via render_crumb() → you get a valid text document.

Stable contract for the v=1.x line. New optional headers and sections may be added in minor releases; the required set is frozen until v=2.0.

headers.description: Key-value pairs from the header zone (lines above the `---` separator). All values are strings — downstream consumers are responsible for coercing numeric or list-shaped headers. Unknown headers SHOULD be preserved and MAY be ignored by consumers.

v.description: Format version. Parsers that conform to this schema MUST accept `1.1` and MAY accept other `1.x` versions per the stability policy in SPEC.md §9.

kind.description: Document kind. One of the six frozen kinds in v=1.x.

source.description: Origin of this crumb — free-form agent identifier, conventionally `tool.role` (e.g. `claude.assistant`, `cursor.agent`, `human.notes`).

project.description: Project or codebase this crumb relates to.

id.description: Stable identifier. Conventionally a short slug; uniqueness scope is up to the emitter.

url.description: Optional link to source material (e.g. issue tracker, PR, design doc).

tags.description: Comma-separated free-form tags. Parsers pass the raw string through; consumers split on commas.

extensions.description: Comma-separated list of namespaced extension identifiers (e.g. `ext.<ns>.<name>.v1`) declared to be in use by this document. See SPEC.md §8.1.

max_total_tokens.description: Optional hint: max token budget for this crumb when packed into a downstream context window.

max_index_tokens.description: Optional hint: max token budget for any index/retrieval summary derived from this crumb.

sections.description: Named body sections keyed by lowercased section name (e.g. `goal`, `context`, `constraints`). Each value is an array of strings, one per raw line in the rendered output. Trailing blank lines within a section are preserved by the parser and stripped by the renderer — consumers that need a single string per section should `'\n'.join(lines).rstrip()`.

kindRequirements.description: Documentation-only: which sections are required for each kind. Enforced by `parse_crumb()` via `EmptySectionError` / `MissingSectionError`, not by this JSON Schema (which intentionally accepts any section name to stay additive across the v=1.x line).
